title: Attach documents to a Company
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to upload and store documents against a Company entity, so that company-level files are centrally organized and retrievable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am viewing a Company record, when I upload a supported file within the 2 MB limit, then the document is stored and associated with that Company; Given a document is attached to a Company, when I open the Company's documents area, then the file is listed with its metadata (name, upload date, uploaded by, expiry date if set) and is downloadable; Given I upload a Company document, when it is saved, then it is subject to the same format, size, categorization, expiry, and role-based access rules as other entities; Given documents belong to my tenant, when I attach or view Company documents, then only my tenant's Company documents are accessible.

title: Attach documents to an Employee
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to upload and store documents against an Employee record, so that employee-specific files are kept with the correct person.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am viewing an Employee record, when I upload a supported file within the 2 MB limit, then the document is stored and associated with that Employee; Given documents exist for an Employee, when I view their profile documents area, then all attached documents are listed with metadata (name, upload date, uploaded by, expiry date if set) and are downloadable; Given I attach an Employee document, when I save it, then I can assign a category and optional expiry date; Given Employee documents exist, when access is evaluated, then only users whose role permits access can view or download them.

title: Attach documents to a Candidate
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to upload and store documents against a Candidate within Talent Acquisition, so that candidate files stay tied to the applicant record.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am viewing a Candidate within Talent Acquisition, when I upload a supported file within the 2 MB limit, then the document is stored and associated with that Candidate; Given documents exist for a Candidate, when I open their record, then the attached files are listed with metadata and are accessible/downloadable; Given I attach a Candidate document, when I save it, then it supports categorization, optional expiry date, and role-based access like other entities; Given Candidate documents belong to my tenant, when accessed, then no cross-tenant exposure occurs.
source: FR 6.22.1 (Supported Entities — Candidate within Talent Acquisition)

title: Restrict uploads to supported file formats
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the system to accept only PDF, JPG/JPEG, DOC/DOCX, XLS/XLSX, PNG, and TXT files, so that only usable document types are stored.
acceptance_criteria: Given I select a file in a supported format (PDF, JPG, JPEG, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX, PNG, TXT), when I upload it, then the upload succeeds; Given I select a file in an unsupported format, when I attempt to upload it, then the upload is rejected with a clear error listing the allowed formats; Given format validation runs, when a file's actual type is checked, then validation is enforced consistently for Company, Employee, and Candidate uploads.

title: Enforce maximum file size
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the system to enforce a 2 MB maximum file size, so that storage stays controlled and uploads remain performant.
acceptance_criteria: Given a file is 2 MB or smaller, when I upload it, then the upload is accepted; Given a file exceeds 2 MB, when I attempt to upload it, then the upload is rejected with a message stating the 2 MB limit; Given the size limit is enforced, when uploads occur on any supported entity (Company, Employee, Candidate), then the 2 MB maximum applies uniformly.
source: FR 6.22.2 (File Support — 2 MB maximum file size)

title: Capture document metadata
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want each document to record metadata such as name, upload date, uploaded by, and expiry date, so that files can be identified and audited.
acceptance_criteria: Given I upload a document, when it is saved, then the name, upload date, and uploaded-by user are recorded automatically; Given I set an expiry date during upload or edit, when the document is saved, then the expiry date is stored as part of its metadata; Given a document has metadata, when I view the document, then name, upload date, uploaded by, and expiry date (if set) are all displayed; Given metadata is captured, when documents are listed, then metadata is available for identification and audit purposes.

title: Track document expiration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to track document expiration, so that I can identify documents that have expired or are approaching expiry.
acceptance_criteria: Given a document has an expiry date, when that date passes, then the document is flagged as expired; Given I review stored documents, when I filter or view by expiry, then expired and upcoming-expiry documents are identifiable; Given documents without an expiry date exist, when expiration tracking runs, then they are treated as non-expiring and not flagged; Given a document is flagged expired, when I view it, then its expired status is clearly indicated alongside its metadata.

title: Categorize documents
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to categorize documents, so that files can be organized and located by type.
acceptance_criteria: Given I upload or edit a document, when I assign a category, then the category is saved with the document; Given documents are categorized, when I browse documents, then I can view or filter them by category; Given categorization is applied, when documents span Company, Employee, and Candidate entities, then categories organize files consistently across those entities.

title: Enforce role-based access to documents
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want document access to be governed by role-based access controls, so that users only see documents they are authorized to view.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user has a role permitting access to a document, when they open the entity, then the document is visible and accessible/downloadable to them; Given a user's role does not permit access, when they view the entity, then the restricted document is not shown or downloadable; Given role-based access is enforced, when access is evaluated, then it applies to Company, Employee, and Candidate documents and combines with tenant isolation so no unauthorized or cross-tenant access occurs.
source: FR 6.22.3 (Document Features — role-based access); FR 6.22.4 (Security — access controls)

title: Secure documents with encryption and tenant isolation
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want documents encrypted at rest and isolated per tenant, so that stored files are protected and never exposed across tenants.
acceptance_criteria: Given a document is stored, when it resides at rest, then it is encrypted; Given documents belong to a specific tenant, when any user accesses documents, then only that tenant's documents are retrievable, with no cross-tenant leakage; Given encryption and isolation are in force, when documents are read or downloaded, then they are decrypted only for authorized users of the owning tenant; Given tenant isolation applies, when documents exist for Company, Employee, and Candidate entities, then the isolation boundary holds across all supported entities.
source: FR 6.22.4 (Security — encryption at rest, tenant isolation, access controls)

title: View own attached documents
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view and download documents attached to my own Employee record, so that I can access my personal files when my role permits.
acceptance_criteria: Given documents are attached to my Employee record, when I open my profile documents area and my role permits access, then I can view the list with metadata (name, upload date, uploaded by, expiry date) and download the files; Given a document on my record is restricted by role-based access, when I view my profile, then that document is not shown or downloadable to me; Given I access my documents, when retrieval occurs, then only documents within my tenant and permitted by my role are returned.
source: FR 6.22.1 (Supported Entities — Employee); FR 6.22.3 (Document Features — role-based access)

title: Upload documents to own record
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to upload supported documents to my own Employee record when permitted, so that I can submit personal files without admin assistance.
acceptance_criteria: Given my role permits self-upload, when I upload a supported file (PDF, JPG/JPEG, DOC/DOCX, XLS/XLSX, PNG, TXT) within the 2 MB limit to my record, then it is stored and associated with me and metadata (name, upload date, uploaded by = me) is recorded; Given I select an unsupported format or a file over 2 MB, when I attempt to upload, then the upload is rejected with a clear error; Given my role does not permit self-upload, when I attempt to add a document, then the action is not available; Given I upload a document, when I save it, then I may set a category and optional expiry date if allowed.

title: Receive alerts for expiring and expired documents
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to be alerted about documents nearing or past their expiry date, so that I can renew or replace them before they lapse.
acceptance_criteria: Given documents have expiry dates, when a document approaches its expiry, then it appears in an upcoming-expiry view or alert so I can act; Given a document's expiry date has passed, when I review documents, then it is surfaced as expired for follow-up; Given multiple documents share expiry status, when I filter by expiry, then I can see all expiring and expired documents across Company, Employee, and Candidate records within my tenant.

title: Manage documents across companies in the group
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to manage and review documents across the companies in my group, so that I have consolidated oversight of document compliance and expiry.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies belong to my group, when I access documents, then I can view and manage documents for entities across those companies subject to role-based access and tenant isolation; Given documents exist across group companies, when I filter by category or expiry, then I can identify expiring, expired, and categorized documents group-wide; Given tenant isolation applies, when I access group documents, then no documents outside my group's tenant scope are exposed.
source: FR 6.22.1; FR 6.22.3 (role-based access, expiration tracking); FR 6.22.4 (tenant isolation)

title: Oversee document compliance across the portfolio
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want portfolio-wide visibility into stored documents and their status, so that I can monitor document management and compliance across all group companies in my portfolio.
acceptance_criteria: Given group companies exist within my portfolio, when I access document oversight, then I can review document presence, categories, and expiry status across the portfolio subject to role-based access; Given documents carry metadata and expiry, when I review the portfolio, then expired and upcoming-expiry documents are identifiable across companies; Given tenant isolation and access controls apply, when I view portfolio documents, then only documents within my authorized portfolio scope are visible with no cross-tenant leakage.
source: FR 6.22.1; FR 6.22.3 (expiration tracking, role-based access); FR 6.22.4 (access controls, tenant isolation)

title: Configure the document category taxonomy
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define and maintain the per-tenant list of document categories as governed configuration, so that document organization matches my company's document types without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open document configuration, when I add, rename, retire, or reorder categories, then the taxonomy is saved as tenant-scoped, versioned, effective-dated config that the categorization feature reads; Given a category is retired, when it is no longer active, then existing documents retain their historical category while it is no longer offered for new uploads; Given the taxonomy changes, when Company, Employee, and Candidate uploads run, then category choices reflect the current configured taxonomy consistently; Given config is tenant-scoped, when I edit categories, then changes apply only within my tenant.

title: Govern allowed formats and size limit as policy config
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the supported file formats and maximum file size to be maintained as governed, versioned policy configuration, so that upload validation rules can be adjusted without redeploying code.
acceptance_criteria: Given a file-upload policy exists, when it defines allowed formats (PDF, JPG/JPEG, DOC/DOCX, XLS/XLSX, PNG, TXT) and the maximum size (2 MB), then upload validation reads these values from config rather than hard-coded rules; Given I update the policy within governance, when the change is saved, then it is versioned and effective-dated and applied to subsequent uploads; Given the policy is tenant-scoped, when validation runs, then the correct tenant policy is enforced for Company, Employee, and Candidate uploads; Given no override exists, when uploads occur, then the platform default policy applies.
source: FR 6.22.2 (File Support — supported formats, 2 MB maximum) (L2 config)

title: Configure role-based access rules for documents
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure which roles may view, download, and upload documents by category, so that access control reflects my company's policy as governed configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open access configuration, when I map roles to document permissions (view, download, upload) per category, then the mapping is saved as tenant-scoped, versioned, effective-dated config that the access-control check reads; Given the access config changes, when users interact with documents, then visibility and download rights reflect the current configuration; Given a role has no permission for a category, when its users view an entity, then documents in that category are neither shown nor downloadable; Given config is combined with tenant isolation, when access is evaluated, then no cross-tenant or unauthorized access occurs.

title: Configure expiry alert lead time
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure how far in advance an expiring-document warning is raised, so that expiry alerts match my company's renewal lead time.
acceptance_criteria: Given expiration tracking is enabled, when I set the warning lead time (e.g. days before expiry) in configuration, then it is saved as tenant-scoped, versioned, effective-dated config; Given the lead time is configured, when a document's expiry falls within that window, then it is classified as upcoming-expiry for alerting; Given the lead time is changed, when tracking next evaluates documents, then the new window governs which documents are flagged; Given no lead time is set, when tracking runs, then the platform default window applies within my tenant.

title: Automated expiry notifications via the notification engine
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the notification engine to generate expiry alerts using configured templates and lead time, so that stakeholders are proactively informed as documents approach or pass expiry.
acceptance_criteria: Given the notification engine evaluates documents on schedule, when a document enters the configured upcoming-expiry window or becomes expired, then the engine generates a notification from the applicable template to the configured recipients; Given a template and lead-time config exist, when the engine runs, then it reads that config rather than hard-coded logic to decide timing, content, and audience; Given documents span Company, Employee, and Candidate entities within a tenant, when notifications are produced, then they respect tenant isolation and role-based access; Given a document is renewed or its expiry cleared before firing, when the engine next evaluates, then no further expiry alert is generated for it.
source: FR 6.22.3 (Document Features — expiration tracking) (L3 Notification/Template engine)

title: Engine-enforced upload validation from policy config
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the upload validation engine to enforce format and size rules by evaluating the governed policy config, so that only compliant files are accepted consistently across all entities.
acceptance_criteria: Given a governed file-upload policy is active, when a file is submitted, then the validation engine evaluates the file's actual type and size against the configured allowed formats and maximum size before storing it; Given a file violates the policy, when validation runs, then the engine rejects the upload and returns a clear error citing the allowed formats or the size limit; Given the policy is updated in config, when the next upload occurs, then the engine applies the new rules without code changes; Given uploads occur on Company, Employee, and Candidate entities, when validation runs, then the engine enforces the same tenant policy uniformly.
source: FR 6.22.2 (File Support — supported formats, 2 MB maximum) (L3 Rules/Forms engine)

title: Search and filter documents in a documents grid
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a documents grid with search, filter, and sort, so that I can quickly locate files by name, category, entity, or expiry status.
acceptance_criteria: Given documents exist across entities in my tenant, when I open the documents grid, then documents are listed with their metadata (name, category, upload date, uploaded by, expiry date, expiry status); Given I enter a search term or apply filters (category, entity type, expiry status), when the grid updates, then only matching documents within my tenant and permitted by my role are shown; Given I sort by a column such as name, upload date, or expiry date, when the grid re-renders, then the ordering reflects my selection; Given I select a document from the grid, when I choose to view or download it, then the file opens or downloads subject to role-based access and tenant isolation.
source: FR 6.22.3 (Document Features — metadata, categorization, expiration tracking) (L4 presentation)

title: Maintain a document type master with applicable modules
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define document types with a description and the modules they apply to, so that documents are categorized consistently and only offered where relevant.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the document type master, when I add a new type with a name, description, and one or more applicable modules (e.g. Employee Management, Organization), then the type is saved and appears in the list showing Type, Description, and Applicable modules; Given a type is scoped to specific modules, when a user uploads a document in a module, then only document types applicable to that module are selectable; Given the master is tenant-scoped, when I manage types, then changes apply only within my tenant.

title: Edit an existing document type
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to edit a document type's description and applicable modules, so that I can keep the taxonomy accurate as needs change.
acceptance_criteria: Given a document type is listed, when I click Edit and change its description or applicable modules, then the updated type is saved and reflected in the list; Given documents already reference the type, when I edit it, then existing documents retain their association while new metadata applies going forward; Given I edit a type, when I save, then validation ensures a name is present.

title: Page through and refresh the document types list
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to paginate and refresh the document types list, so that I can review all defined types across many entries.
acceptance_criteria: Given more document types exist than fit one page, when I use pagination controls (next/last) or Refresh, then the list reloads showing the current page of types with their Type, Description, and Applicable modules; Given items are displayed, when the list loads, then a count such as 'Displaying items 1 - 10 of N' is shown; Given I refresh, when new types were added by another admin, then they appear in the reloaded list.

title: Register required certificates with issuing authority
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define required certificates along with their issuing authority, so that certification requirements are tracked for employees.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the Required Certificates screen, when I add a new certificate with a certificate name and authority name, then the certificate is saved and listed; Given certificates exist, when I view the list, then each row shows Certificate name and Authority name; Given the master is tenant-scoped, when I add certificates, then they are available only within my tenant.

title: See an empty-state when no required certificates exist
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the certificates list to indicate when nothing is configured, so that I know setup is still pending.
acceptance_criteria: Given no required certificates have been added, when I view the list, then a clear 'No records to display' message is shown with a count of 0; Given I then add a certificate, when the list reloads, then the empty-state is replaced by the certificate rows; Given the empty-state is shown, when I click Add new certificate, then I can begin configuration.

title: Assign a document custodian position per document type
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to assign a custodian position responsible for each document type, so that ownership of documents is clearly accountable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open Document Custodian, when I add a new custodian mapping selecting a document type and a custodian position, then the mapping is saved and listed with Document type, Custodian position, and Applicability; Given a custodian is assigned to a document type, when documents of that type are handled, then the responsible custodian position is identifiable; Given the mapping is tenant-scoped, when I manage custodians, then changes apply only within my tenant.

title: Scope custodian applicability by org dimensions
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to scope a document custodian by Location, Department, Position, and Employee class, so that the right custodian applies within the correct organizational context.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create or edit a custodian mapping, when I set applicability by Location, Department, Position, and/or Employee class, then the mapping applies only within that scope; Given multiple custodian mappings exist for a document type, when applicability is evaluated, then the mapping matching the employee's org context governs; Given a custodian's details are viewed, when I open the record, then its Document type, Custodian position, and Applicability are displayed.

title: Filter and reset the document custodian list
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to filter custodian mappings by Location, Department, Position, and Employee class and reset the filters, so that I can quickly find relevant custodian records.
acceptance_criteria: Given custodian mappings exist, when I set one or more filter values and click Search, then only matching custodian records are shown; Given filters are applied, when I click Reset, then the filters clear and the full custodian list is shown; Given I refresh the list, when it reloads, then the latest custodian assignments are displayed.

title: Oversee custodian assignments across the group
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to review document custodian assignments across the companies in my group, so that every document type has accountable ownership group-wide.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies belong to my group, when I review custodian mappings, then I can see which document types have assigned custodian positions across those companies subject to role-based access; Given a document type has no custodian in a company, when I review coverage, then the gap is identifiable for follow-up; Given tenant isolation applies, when I review custodians, then no mappings outside my group's scope are exposed.

title: Publish policy documents with effective dates and applicability
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to publish company policy documents with a name, effective-from/effective-till dates, and applicability, so that employees can access current company policies.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the Policy Document screen, when I add a new policy with a name, effective dates, and applicability, then the policy is saved and listed; Given a policy has an effective period, when active policies are displayed, then only policies within their effective-from/effective-till range are treated as active; Given a policy is applicable to a group of employees, when applicability is set, then only those employees see it as applicable.

title: Exclude position levels from a policy's applicability
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to exclude specific position levels from a policy, so that a policy applies only to the intended audience.
acceptance_criteria: Given I configure a policy's applicability, when I add excluded position levels, then employees at those levels are not treated as subject to the policy; Given excluded position levels are set, when the policy is previewed or viewed, then its applicability and excluded position levels are displayed; Given exclusions change, when applicability is next evaluated, then the updated audience governs which employees the policy applies to.

title: Preview and view applicable policy documents
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to preview and view policy documents that apply to me, so that I understand which company policies I am subject to.
acceptance_criteria: Given policies are published and applicable to me, when I open a policy and click View or Preview, then the policy content, applicability, and excluded position levels are shown; Given a policy excludes my position level or falls outside its effective period, when I view policies, then it is not presented as applicable to me; Given policies belong to my tenant, when I access them, then only my tenant's policies within my role scope are shown.

title: Filter policies by status and search by name
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to filter policies by Active or Inactive and search by name, then reset, so that I can quickly locate the relevant policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given policies exist, when I select Active or Inactive, enter a name, and click Search, then only matching policies are listed; Given I have applied filters, when I click Reset, then the filters clear and the full policy list is shown; Given a policy's effective dates determine its status, when I filter by Active, then only policies currently within their effective period appear.
